summary: The notebook implements a spatio-temporal trajectory prediction model for the NFL Big Data Bowl by combining a deterministic geometric baseline with a custom Transformer architecture. It computes physics-based endpoint predictions for receivers and defenders, then trains a model to predict the residual corrections needed for complex interactions like coverage and collisions. The pipeline includes extensive feature engineering (route clustering, GNN-lite neighbor embeddings, temporal lags/EMAs) and uses a GroupKFold cross-validation strategy with a time-decayed Huber loss. Readers should take away how geometric priors can simplify sequence modeling tasks and how residual learning combined with spatial embeddings improves trajectory forecasting.
competition_flow: Loads weekly CSV input/output data, computes geometric baselines and spatial-temporal features, trains a custom ST-Transformer with GroupKFold, and averages fold predictions to generate a submission.
data_reading: Reads training input and output CSVs from /kaggle/input/nfl-big-data-bowl-2026-prediction/train/ using pd.read_csv, concatenating weekly files.
data_processing: Handles missing values via mean imputation, pads short sequences to window size 10, clips coordinates to field bounds, applies StandardScaler per fold, and converts Polars DataFrames to Pandas for inference.
features_engineering: Geometric endpoint features (vector, distance, required velocity/acceleration, alignment)
GNN-lite neighbor embeddings (weighted ally/opp distance/velocity aggregates)
Opponent proximity/closing speed, mirror WR offsets, and time-progress features
models: STTransformer (custom PyTorch module with Transformer encoder, attention pooling, and ResidualMLP head)
loss_functions: TemporalHuber (Huber loss with exponential time decay weighting)
ensembling: Averages predictions across all trained fold models and clips final coordinates to field boundaries.
insights: Geometric and physics-based rules capture the majority of player movement, allowing the model to focus on learning complex corrections.
Route clustering and GNN-lite embeddings effectively encode spatial context without heavy graph computation.
Time-decayed loss prioritizes accuracy at later prediction horizons.
